title: Reuse connections through a properly sized pool
description: Use keep-alive and a bounded connection pool so calls avoid handshake cost without leaking or exhausting sockets.
body: Use when a client makes many calls to the same host. Do not open a fresh connection per request (handshake and TIME_WAIT overhead) and do not leave the pool unbounded or fail to return/close connections (socket leak, CLOSE_WAIT buildup). Actions: enable keep-alive, size max connections per host and total, set idle timeouts and max lifetime, and ensure response bodies are fully read/closed so connections return to the pool. Validation signals include connection reuse (few new handshakes under load), a stable socket count, and no descriptor leak over time. Risk boundary: an undersized pool serializes traffic and an unbounded one exhausts file descriptors; size it against load and verify under sustained traffic.
